title: Vulca PPT Run 1.5 Product Lab
audience: Product, growth, and engineering reviewers deciding whether evidence-backed design memory improves generated PPT output.

slides.title: PPT Run 1.5 Product Lab
slides.claim: This run tests whether design evidence changes generated presentation output.
slides.proof_object: Three-arm experiment label with not-run status and public publishing block.

slides.title: Brief Cockpit
slides.claim: The commercial brief becomes a visible control surface before generation starts.
slides.proof_object: Editable brief cockpit showing audience, goal, constraints, decision, and output promise.

slides.title: Source and Tutorial Intake
slides.claim: Reference analysis and tutorial rules are separated before they become memory.
slides.proof_object: Intake matrix mapping source roles, tutorial notes, allowed use, and do-not-copy boundaries.

slides.title: Design Memory Compiler
slides.claim: Evidence becomes strict memory fields that can drive slide primitives.
slides.proof_object: Compiler view linking evidence_id to design_rule, slide_primitive, and QA signal.

slides.title: Skill Workflow Surface
slides.claim: The Vulca PPT skill translates memory into a controlled generation sequence.
slides.proof_object: Workflow rail from intake to outline, code generation, render check, review, and gate.

slides.title: Code Generation Anatomy
slides.claim: Generated slides should expose editable primitives instead of rasterized composition.
slides.proof_object: Annotated native-shape anatomy of claim, proof object, evidence tag, and QA marker.

slides.title: Prompt-Only vs Full Vulca
slides.claim: The comparison should show whether memory changes structure, hierarchy, and proof density.
slides.proof_object: Before-after comparison delta for prompt-only and full Vulca outputs.

slides.title: Ablation Proof
slides.claim: The negative control should fail visibly without relying on malformed artifacts.
slides.proof_object: bad-data arm delta showing structurally valid corrupted rules and expected QA catch.

slides.title: QA and Publish Gate
slides.claim: Public release is blocked until provenance, render, comparison, and review evidence exist.
slides.proof_object: Gate checklist with not-run status, missing evidence slots, and blocked public publishing state.

slides.title: Product Decision
slides.claim: The run should end with a decision on whether design memory is a productized advantage.
slides.proof_object: Decision table linking scores, risks, next experiment, and release recommendation.
